reviews.review_text:
EA and brapcap don't even know how to remaster 2009 2d mobile games without bloating filesizes, using ai and introducing 6 gorillion new issues that never even existed in the original (and obviously charging more money for it)

just get this instead, pvz replanted is soulless digital slop

Just an absolutely amazing and classic tower defense game. I know my review is belated years after I beat the main campaign on PC but I have loved the franchise for 10 years and am mad at myself for not having reviewed this game sooner. The gameplay is so simple and after playing the main game through a couple of times the gameplay loop can become quite mindless but it is still peak. I think the simplicity allows for all of the creative challenge videos that I love watching on YouTube. So many things about the game aren’t just good: they are memorable. All of the plants have personality and they aren’t all just reskins of each other. The soundtrack never gets old and is immediately recognizable. The art direction is nothing to sleep on as well, the design of both the plants and the zombies are full of charm and are cute. And there’s a zombie on your lawn is an absolute banger and you can’t convince me otherwise. Just please play this game, it is one of the most memorable indie games imo

A good bit of fun. Worth a play. This is an old game and I remember first playing it over a decade ago. It's before the great enshitification. It's not super long, it's decent and has it's challenges.

Also, it contains only the second best Laura Shigihara song. To The Moon is still unassailable. Still. Worth a play.

been playing since i was 3. For context I'm 15. I have probably beat this game about 32 times in total. On laptops, tablet, phone, Chromebook, Xbox 360, steam, etc. I find this game fun to play randomly throughout the day whenever I think about what to play. Once I get close to beating the game I start grinding all of the game modes and puzzles until i beat them all. Then i do campaign again until i get the yeti achievement. Once I do that, I finish the campaign, grow my tree taller, and work on my plants in the Zen garden. truly never boring unless I'm playing the fog levels (I dont like fog levels) And i just recently realized you can type things like "daises" and the zombies will walk with a trail of daises behind them lol. Anyway, still not getting the replanted version on here because it is straight disrespect to the original artists by using Ai. I truly cant stand EA with their microtransaction on mobile, and their Constant ads every 10 seconds. SO if you want to play PVZ, you should play on here or on the Xbox 360. I love this game truly, just not EA, I hate EA.

Out of every game this company has made, this is the best one by a long stretch. 

Around 13 years ago my uncle pirated the game for me. I had never heard of the game before and it was one of the 4 games he installed on that PC. As soon as I started playing I was addicted. I remember that after the first time of beating survival I told myself that when I was older I would give the developers the money they deserved. I eventually did and I do not regret it. I've beaten this game around 5 times already and it has never once gotten old.

Pop Cap, thank you from the bottom of my heart. I hope I can one day make an amazing game like this one
Could be higher resolution. Otherwise its the same fun game I played years ago on my phone.

Popcap really struck gold on how to make something infinitely addictive and super exciting without making it too mindless or too overwhelming to play here. The levels go from super chill to chaotic crazy fast and i love it. Combine that with a surprising amount of depth and gameplay where if you make one mistake in your planting you can get ♥♥♥♥♥♥ over and you can see why this is seen as a classic.

The art style is super adorable and plays this awesome mix between gnarly and disgusting and adorable and silly, and you team up with your schizophrenic neighbor which is arguably the best part aside from the dude who leaked Forsaken. 

Ultimately the sellout to EA was a monkey's paw because while it did give us some absolutely brilliant side games like the Garden Warfare series we will never see a true sequel to this game that isn't a mining ground for extra money for a company that already makes way too much from the Battlefield series. There's also replanted but let's ignore that it ever happened.

[h1]Still good[/h1]

I remember Zombie on your lawn song and such when it just arrived
And it's over 15 years ago, the game is still nice to play
A classic game that holds up perfectly to this day. Challenging but fair, creative but concise. Play this version and try not to think about what has become of PopCap since
The games great but it can get a bit repetitive after a while, Add sex

At first, I LOVED IT! But now, For no reason, I'm not really liking it! And yes, due to my big screen, I have lot's of a Boardless black background whille playing it! but yes, I still like but not that much now!
Great Game, beautiful artwork, such an enjoyable classic!
This is one of the best games I’ve ever played, it’s highly addictive and something great to zone out and play for multiple hours.
A legendary, iconic childhood game - relax and unwind

after 100 years i finally went back and got the damn "Better Off Dead" achievement, and only barely. whoever decided it should be a 10-streak is evil

i also just replayed the entire game on Nintendo DS which was an interesting experience with reference to framerate

anyway fun game. too bad about that repainted editon though lmao you'd think it'd be impossible to mess up and yet they found a way

If you're genuinely reading the reviews section of PVZ GOTY, you're either clueless about the franchise or just being funny.

If you are the former, Plants Vs. Zombies was the game to popularize the tower defense genre, and to this day, remains an absolute classic- fun gameplay, amazing music, and generally has aged like wine.

The game's $10, you need to buy it.

No, I actually might go back to Plants vs. Zombies.

Why? So the peashooter has many forms: fire, ice, electric, shoot, even shadow. But recently there's been new mods that really need to be added to the game. So there's three and the first one is bendy, he shoots wrenches, look at this. Oh that's sick, but why not ink? Dude wait, that's actually a good idea, but look at this one. Oh they should actually add that. Yeah, the game would go viral but look at this, it's John Pork. What? I'm just kidding, actually look at this one. Is that an enderman? Yeah, it's an ender pea, it shoots minecraft blocks!
My father is not what people would describe as a gamer, and yet he completed this game. Be like my dad and finish this game, or he will be disappointed in you.
i loved this game back than and still do very classic and calm to play nothing ever will beat the classic
PvZ GOTY is absolutely worth the money... and don't waste your money on the Replanted version. This game has all the charm and minigames for 1/4 the price.
this game carried me through my childhood for many years. it is simple and easy to understand and i strongly recdomend it to all gamers around the world
Classic tower defense game. I would recommend this game to anyone. Great price and amazing gameplay. Also great original concept of plants vs zombies. Solid 8/10
Really enjoyed this game to the fullest. I was told this game allowed you to make your own zombie so I bought it. Anyways really fun reminds me of the old pvz
Really good game, lots to do and it's not that expensive. 100% it, it was fun

This game has played such a large roll in my gaming life. It was my favorite game for many years. I have played the gamer on many platforms, however this is my first time owning it on PC. I cannot recommend this game enough to anyone who likes tower defence games. Plants vs Zombies is in my top 10 games of all time and definitely worth five dollars even after 17 years. 10/10 absolute cinema.
Better than the Mobile version and Replanted. The only good thing about those versions is being able to replay levels by selecting.
Played PVZ 2, sucked, grabbed this pessimisticlly thinking the haze of nestalgia would lift and ruin it too. It didn't, it was just as fun as back in the day. 10/10 or whatever the button is.
the graphics are a little dated, but its way better than ai slop and alot of content for 5 bucks this is a must for laptops

a classic that still holds up

wonderful and fun tower defense
inventive
9/10
il go over whats good and its minor flaws


not to hard not to easy
good difficulty curve
good amount of levels theres no bloat in this game at all
you unluck plants frequintly so theres always something new
each world has a unique and f un gimick that spices up gameplay, the zombies all bring there own mechanicks,

musick is amazing, 

art is good

gameplay is fun, intuative, and rewarding,

levels are paced very well, you have enot time to stratagise, while alsonot having to wait,

well well woerth the price

the devs where indie at the time of making it so theres loads of charm, and love and care put into this game 

(if you type moutsche the zombies get fake moustaches)

i havent played modded pvz, but there are some popluer mods out there, im not sure how easy it is to mod though, theres quite a lot of mod playing veidoes so id say not hard

bounus conte and minigames are sooo funnn

each plant is unique

its 4.99 just buy ittttt
now to the flaws wich are few

there could be a bit more levels, 50 levesl is a good amount if the game had 100 it would be to much, i think 50 levels and 25 bounus levels would be a good amount

the bigger issue with levels is the grind

the grind isent bad in pvz, the issue is when the grind happens (not actualyy a grindy game but if you wantto unock all the plants in a normall playthrough there will be some grind, its not to much, (unlike future pvz games (looking at you garden warfair 2)
at the end, at the end of the game is when you can really start making money, you unlock the zen garden, marigold, and bounus content at the end or near the ensd of the adventure mode, they are the mmain sources of coins, the shop has loads of items by the end of the game, and so it is a little grindy if you dont pase yourself, se if there was 2 bounus levels you could buy more plants in the zen garden, get some coins from minigmes, and farm a few coins with margolds, while still playing more levels, and while adventure mode can be replayed with increased difficulty its not reeally enoth to justify grinding for every purchasable plant, luckily you dont have to, if you dont want a few of them you can just not buy them, and honestly the ones you buy for coins are ussualy less fun.. kind of, its just the game is so  good but you finish the level content before finishing the plant content, and the grind is very much worth it, or if you replay adventure mode the grind isent bad but that also means youl have to replay the first few worlds without to many new plants, although the plants from later levels in advernture mode round 1 can be used so you do have new options, the purchasable plants youl have to choose only a few up until the end of replaying adventure, now survival mode does have a lot of replayabilutly an d coin farming potential, 

another problem is the lack of level select, i originaly played the mobile version (btw this version is way better if you havent bought it because you own the mobile version you should buy i) in the mobile version you can select levels from adventure mode to replay them, and after completing adventure mode you can play through it agin on harder diddiculty each time you completea level on adventure or adventure to you unlock it in the level select, goty edition doesent work that way, there is no level select, i thought mabye i have to beat adventure mode to unlock it but nope its just not in the game, meaning you have to play adventure 2 in order, wth no option to replay the adventure 1 (for earlier levels this doesnt matter becuase by now theyd be to easy but for lator levels id like to replay them) this makes adventure 2 just adventure 1 but harder oh and also crazy dave atoselects three plants at random, the point is to force you to tey out new stragies and combos, like if dave selects coffe bean, i might as well use it so il select some mushrooms wich helps to shake things up, the issue is its random, he could select 3 bad plants that dont work with anaything, (dave wont select plants thatcant be used in the level eg he wont select aquatick plants in levels with no water) so you can just get duds and instead of it being hmm let me rethink  my startageyits i guess i can bring less plants, also if you reload the leavel the 3 selected plnts change, i think having the crazy dave feuture as oprrtial, would be fun and also preset the plants he chooses, so the choices are actualy intresting, 

adventure round 2 doesent fel like you have much choice in it ianly becuase its once againnlinear wihout a level select and wit h a smetimes fun sometimes anoying gimick, why not have a level selector

some more minor issues include,

no widescreen suporrt, to me i barely noticed it this game is fine wiyhout it,

low resoulition, also dident notice, sometimes i did notice and i found the low res charming, its 720p i beliave, the art is very detailed though so its good, 

after a few playthrougs the adventure mode gets a bit easy, there is advernture 2, but minigames and puzzels dont have hard modeas and some of them re  little eassy after plying once or twice, they are minigames though so there short and not proper levesl so they get a pass

mian issue is just the level select 

i talked a lot about issues but they are really minor, (evel select is the only one that really matters)

i love this game

in summary

content is sufficent plenaty of bounus content, dificulty good, good difficulty curve, unique mechanicks, good art, great music, content at the ned of the game is a bit lobsided, its mainly replay adventure butttt,, bounus content, and a lot of cool stuff you need coins to buy, zomibes are uniw=que, game is paced very well, well worth price, no level select bog sit doen a little, could be a few more levesl,

This is my first ever Steam game. I have fond memories playing this on the family desktop whenever the internet would go out. I was so excited to learn I could play it again. The mobile version just isn't the same. It's changed a lot and I hate in app purchases. Luckily, GOTY edition is every bit as good as I remember. Zombatars, the Tree of Wisdom, Stinky the snail...
God I had no idea how much of a grind this game would be after completing the main adventure. This capitalistic money ♥♥♥♥ if overrated. Anyways. Utter classic
Its a good Tower defense game pretty good would recommend and its only 5 dollars so why now kinda short but its fun

This game is an absolute classic and it sucks that EA and Popcap don't seem to want to give the series the love and respect it deserves. 

The music is really good, I like how dynamic it is, the gameplay is actually quite fun, and it's honestly just fun. You actually get quite a lot of content in this, which makes it worth the price, in my opinion.

Would highly recommend.
Humorous, challenging and fun. Just very annoying to get it working on modern systems.

I can't tell you the number of times that I played this game from the beginning to the end and on top of that I even played plants vs zombies 2 before the pre modern era changes where the developers added arena and leveling system, but that's a conversation for another day. This is the best plants vs zombies official game exists that were made by the original developers another thing I didn't like is that it doesn't have all the achievements I thought it would have as I saw in the past in the mobile plants vs zombies game before the ads came in, but it's fine. You won't regret playing this version of the game!

Plants vs. Zombies is a classic. I’ll never forget the kid on the bus who was playing it on his iPad (bro really put me on). I never really got into the later games, as they were expensive and on consoles I didn’t have yet, but man, is this game a classic. I still prefer the mobile app versions of the game, but that may just be nostalgia bias.

I hate that EA fired the creator of the game after acquiring PopCap. The studio is now a former shell of what it used to be and hasn’t had a hit in over a decade outside of the PVZ IP. I'm not surprised, since EA ruins everything they touch, killing or neglecting their beloved IPs. And I’m not even hating; they apparently have made a “Replanted” version of the game. I might try it out, ngl. But if you're anything like me and are going to buy this or the “Replanted” game for nostalgia, wait for a sale, especially for the original.
This game is the GOAT of tower defence games. It has magic to it that no other TD can replicate. I'd highly recommened this to anyone who enjoys these types of games.

Still my favourite game of all time I finished the game on my tablet, phone and PC defenitely worth buying even without the promotion  it took me 41 hours to finish all the extra levels and adventure 2 and another 3
hours to finish the zen garden this game is so peak bro worth every single penny

Literally one of the best games ever made. Easily my most favorite tower defense game. Buy this over Replanted every day of the week. THIS is the definitive way to play this game. Great for everyone ranging from sweaty completionists (me) to grandmas who love candy crush. Give this game a shot if you haven't already! :)
The pacing is amazing, it really lets you see how your defences grow through out a level, adds alot more replayablity in my eyes.

What can I say about this game that hasn't been said again and again for the past 17 years. This game is amazing, from the art style to the simple yet engaging gameplay loop, to each new level that introduces new mechanics to keep things interesting, to the insanely iconic soundtrack. This game also hold a special place in my heart as one of my favorite childhood games, and one I still enjoy playing as an adult. I cannot recommend this enough, the only downside I can possibly imaging is giving money to EA.

This isn't also a buggy mess like the remastered. This games design and charm also cannot be beaten. Also this game is great if you want to play pvz fusion i also recommend. Please buy this 5€ master peace.

Plants vs. Zombies is the definition of a "perfect" casual game. The premise is simple yet addictive: you defend your home from a quirky zombie invasion using a variety of specialized plants. From the iconic Peashooter to the explosive Cherry Bomb, the strategy depth is surprisingly rich despite the easy-to-learn mechanics.

The game’s charm lies in its vibrant art style, catchy soundtrack, and the perfect balance of humor and challenge. Whether you're fighting through the daytime levels, the fog, or the final rooftop battle, every stage feels fresh. It’s a rare gem that appeals to both hardcore strategists and casual players looking for a fun time.

This game is incredibly nostalgic. Even though it’s nearly 20 years old, it still holds a special charm. I used to play a cracked version of this game back in the day, but today I decided to officially buy it—partly to support the developer, and partly to relive those childhood memories.

Overrall: 10/10
You have to try it!!

This is an absolute timeless classic that, even years after its release, remains the gold standard of the tower defense genre. Plants vs. Zombies GOTY Edition is a perfect blend of addictive gameplay, whimsical charm, and brilliant design that simply never gets old. What makes this game so special is its incredible sense of progression, you start with simple pea-shooters in your backyard and quickly find yourself defending rooftops in the middle of the night or navigating fog-covered pools with a massive arsenal of unique plants. The game’s balance is flawless, offering an experience that is accessible enough for anyone to pick up while providing surprising strategic depth in the later levels and survival modes. The visual style is bursting with personality, featuring zombies that are more comical than scary, which gives the game that iconic PopCap charm that feels rare in today’s gaming landscape. The soundtrack is legendary, with every track perfectly capturing the quirky energy of the garden warfare. Beyond the main campaign, the sheer amount of content in the GOTY edition from the Zen Garden and Puzzle modes to the hilarious mini-games ensures there is always something fun to come back to. Crazy Dave remains one of the most memorable NPCs in gaming history, adding a layer of humor that makes every interaction a delight. It is a rare gem that appeals to all ages, proving that a simple concept executed with passion and creativity can result in a masterpiece. Whether you are chasing achievements or just looking for the perfect comfort game, this is a mandatory addition to any Steam library. It is pure, unadulterated fun from start to finish.

Actual masterpiece. Probably one of the best games of all time, though that may just be the nostalgia speaking. Either way, this is one game everyone should play at least once. In fact, you should try most of Popcap's releases while you're at it.

Only complaints are that the mobile version has more achievements and that you cannot select the level to play in story mode. Everything else is more enjoyable on the computer.

Plants VS Zombies is simple conept made with care and passion, Thanks to this it has aged like fine vine.
